name: Ecce Homo Trail
name_original: Parque Natural Los Cerros – circular por sendas a la cima del Ecce-Homo
description: This moderate 15.0-mile mountain bike route in Parque Natural Los Cerros offers panoramic views and varied terrain to the Ecce-Homo summit.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails in Parque Natural Los Cerros offer a diverse and complex topography, featuring an extensive network of routes. The region is characterized by dramatic limestone cliffs, dry streams, and extensive pine forests, providing varied terrain for mountain biking. Along the Henares riverbank, riverine vegetation adds to the ecological diversity. This natural park serves as the main green area of Alcalá de Henares, with varied elevation ensuring both rewarding climbs and exciting downhill sections. Best mountain bike trails in Parque Natural Los Cerros The most popular mountain bike trail is Ecce Homo Trail, a 15.0 miles (24.1 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 24 minutes to complete, incorporating ascents to Mount Ecce Homo for elevated views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is El Raso Lagoon – Palacio de Aldovea loop from Los Hueros, a difficult 32.3 miles (52.0 km) path. This route traverses diverse landscapes, offering an engaging experience for riders seeking varied terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Gravel Slope – Ciudad del Aire loop from Alcalá de Henares Universidad, a 27.0 miles (43.5 km) trail leading through varied topography, often completed in about 3 hours 23 minutes. Mountain biking in Parque Natural Los Cerros is defined by limestone cliffs, pine forests, and routes along the Henares river. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging routes with technical descents. The routes in Parque Natural Los Cerros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 650 reviews. More than 5000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Parque Natural Los Cerros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Parque Natural Los Cerros?
A: Parque Natural Los Cerros offers a diverse and complex topography. You'll encounter dramatic limestone cliffs, dry streams, and extensive pine forests. Routes also run along the Henares riverbank, providing varied riverine vegetation. The park's varied elevation ensures both rewarding climbs and exciting downhill sections, including technical descents known as "trialeras".

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Parque Natural Los Cerros?
A: There are nearly 280 mountain bike trails in Parque Natural Los Cerros. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 67 easy, 176 moderate, and 36 difficult trails available for exploration.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Parque Natural Los Cerros?
A: Yes, Parque Natural Los Cerros offers 67 easy mountain bike trails, providing suitable options for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These paths allow riders to enjoy the natural beauty without overly challenging terrain.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Parque Natural Los Cerros features 36 difficult trails. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19563567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Raso Lagoon – Palacio de Aldovea loop from Los Hueros&lt;/a&gt;, a 52.0 km path that traverses diverse landscapes and offers an engaging experience for those seeking varied and demanding terrain.

Q: What are the best times of year for mountain biking in Parque Natural Los Cerros?
A: The park is generally suitable for mountain biking throughout the year. However, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures. Summer can be hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable, while winter rides are possible but may require checking for any specific weather-related trail conditions.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails in Parque Natural Los Cerros offer views of diverse landscapes, including limestone cliffs, pine forests, and the Henares river. Prominent features include Mount Ecce Homo, which offers elevated views. You can also explore historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2393087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Alcalá la Vieja Fortress&lt;/a&gt;, or natural formations such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2391975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malvecino Summit&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2391657" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gigantones Cave&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any loop trails for mountain biking in the park?
A: Yes, many trails in Parque Natural Los Cerros are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38208775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel Slope – Ciudad del Aire loop from Alcalá de Henares Universidad&lt;/a&gt; is a 43.5 km trail that takes about 3 hours 23 minutes to complete, leading through varied topography.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Parque Natural Los Cerros?
A: The mountain bike trails in Parque Natural Los Cerros are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 650 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the extensive network of trails, and the beautiful natural surroundings, including the pine forests and scenic viewpoints.

Q: Are there any facilities or amenities near the mountain bike trails?
A: The park is located near Alcalá de Henares, offering access to various amenities. Within the park, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1457141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Interpretation Center and Fountain of the Alcalá Hills&lt;/a&gt;, which provides information and facilities. For more extensive services like cafes, pubs, and accommodation, the nearby city of Alcalá de Henares is a convenient option.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails typically take to complete in Parque Natural Los Cerros?
A: Trail durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963622728/ecce-homo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ecce Homo Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 24.1 km route, typically takes around 2 hours 24 minutes. Longer, more difficult routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19563567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Raso Lagoon – Palacio de Aldovea loop from Los Hueros&lt;/a&gt; can take approximately 4 hours.

Q: Can I find trails that offer elevated views in the park?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate ascents to prominent features like Mount Ecce Homo, which is known for offering elevated and panoramic views of the surrounding area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963622728/ecce-homo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ecce Homo Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, specifically designed to include these scenic viewpoints.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that follow the Henares river?
A: Yes, the park's proximity to the Henares river means that many routes offer pleasant sections along its banks. These riverine trails add to the ecological diversity of the ride and provide a different landscape experience compared to the higher, more arid areas of the park.

faqs.question: How long does it take to mountain bike Parque Natural Los Cerros – circular por sendas a la cima del Ecce-Homo?
faqs.answer: This circular mountain bike route typically takes around 2 hours and 24 minutes to complete. The total distance is approximately 24.14 km (15 miles).

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is generally considered moderate. While much of it is 'easy to perform' and suitable for a wide range of riders, there are technical sections, including descents with loose stones and some uphill portions that demand good physical condition. An experienced local rider advises caution on certain 'exposed' sections with drops. It's a great option for those looking to progress beyond absolute beginner trails.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is accessible from the entrance of the Parque Natural de Los Cerros in Alcalá de Henares. While specific parking details aren't provided, it's a popular area, so parking is generally available near the park entrances.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: You can expect an exciting ride over varied terrain, including 'sendas' (paths) and singletrack sections. There are segments with loose stones, especially on descents, and the route traverses ravines and passes through pleasant pine forests like the Tabla Pintora Pine Forest. Some areas even feature 'peraltes' (berms) for added fun.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a mix of natural beauty and historical interest. You'll encounter highlights such as the Camino de los Tarayes, the challenging Steep Section on Camino de la Puerta Verde, and the Alto de los Reventones. The Ecce-Homo summit is a major highlight, offering extensive panoramic views. You'll also pass by the remains of the ancient Arabian castle of Alcalá la Vieja and various caves.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints along the trail?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The Ecce-Homo summit is the primary viewpoint, providing breathtaking panoramic views of Alcalá de Henares, the Madrid skyline, and the entire Henares valley. Other excellent spots include the Panoramic View of Alcalá de Henares from Los Cerros Natural Park and the View of Alcalá de Henares from Los Cerros Natural Park.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route utilizes sections of several official trails within the park. You'll ride parts of the Ruta del Ecce-Homo, Ruta de la Puerta Verde, Ruta del Castillo Árabe, and Ruta de los Tarayes, among others like Camino de Anchuelo and Camino de la Barca.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Parque Natural Los Cerros generally welcomes dogs, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife and other trail users. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for Parque Natural Los Cerros?
faqs.answer: No, the Parque Natural Los Cerros is a free mountain bike circuit, and there are no permits or entrance fees required to access the trails.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for mountain biking in this region, offering pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. Summers can be very hot, so if you ride then, it's best to go early in the morning to avoid the peak heat. Winters are usually mild but can have occasional cold snaps.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, we recommend bringing plenty of water, especially on warmer days. Essential bike repair tools, a first-aid kit, appropriate cycling attire, and sun protection are also advisable. Don't forget your camera for the panoramic views!
